description:
Add a landing policy for delegated workers that stops after validation/landability checks and leaves the worktree unmerged for human testing when desired.

Scope:
- support a deferred landing mode alongside the current auto-land flow
- allow the orchestrator to validate the delegated result, confirm its current mergeability, and then intentionally hold the worktree unmerged
- preserve enough runtime state that a later explicit command or natural-language direction can tell the orchestrator to bring the work in
- when later told to land, keep the LLM/orchestrator responsible for the full landing flow: confirm the work is still valid in context, fast-forward merge when possible, or drive rebase/update/fix work when drift/conflicts require it
- surface truthful operator-facing states for 'validated and held', 'ready to land', 'needs refresh', and 'needs attention'
- add tests covering hold-then-land flows

Goal: delegated work should be able to pause in a clean, reviewable, testable pre-merge state without abandoning orchestrator ownership of the final merge-back.
title: Support deferred landing after validation so delegated work can be tested before merge-back
